hero_subtitle: SFTY enables MLROs to securely manage escalations and audit-ready evidence with granular access controls designed for institutional digital asset compliance.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Access control gaps expose operational risk
problem_description: Without precise role-based permissions, MLROs lack timely and controlled access to high-risk cases and escalation workflows. This slows review efficiency, weakens audit trails, and increases complexity in managing compliance evidence and sensitive reports across the enterprise.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY provides role-based permissions that centralize MLRO access to high-risk cases, escalation workflows and sensitive reports while preserving audit logs and evidence for transparent, efficient compliance operations.
workflow_title: From role-based access to case review
workflow_description: SFTY assigns MLRO permissions to control access, route high-risk cases, preserve evidence and manage compliance reviews within a secure audit-log environment.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another dashboard. Controlled access governance
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides a provider-neutral layer that structures MLRO workflows with role-based permissions, audit-ready evidence access, and controlled oversight over high-risk compliance cases.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides VASPs with a secure, role-based compliance layer that preserves audit evidence and supports governed access for bank due diligence and enterprise deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support MLRO role-based case access control
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how SFTY manages MLRO access to high-risk cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve MLRO review and decision history
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Explain provider orchestration for MLRO escalation and oversight
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for MLRO access control?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides MLROs with role-based access to cases, escalations and reports, supporting controlled oversight of high-risk compliance activities within regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage high-risk case access?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures high-risk case workflows with configurable permissions, escalation paths and secure access logs to support MLROs review and manage cases efficiently and compliantly.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case histories and supporting evidence in audit-ready formats, enabling thorough review and traceability for regulatory inspections and internal governance.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects and orchestrates outputs from KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one controlled compliance operating layer without disrupting existing stacks.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace underlying providers but integrates their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports for scalable, auditable compliance operations.
final_title: Provide MLROs controlled access to high-risk cases
final_description: Review how SFTY structures role-based permissions, escalation workflows and audit-ready access logs for MLRO compliance oversight.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
